justifications.clarity: Prose flows naturally from narrative opening (Tetlock) through formal definitions; the new LSTM and Transformer sections maintain precise, unambiguous notation with well-labeled environments; transitions between context and deepdive blocks are smooth.
justifications.rigor: Theorem 1 (vanishing gradient bound) and Proposition 1 (sqrt(d_k) variance normalisation) both include complete proofs; LSTM Definition specifies all six gate equations; regulatory claims cite EU AI Act articles and SEC 2023 guidance, though article numbers could benefit from primary-source footnotes.
justifications.completeness: All eight learning objectives are now covered: LO1 (history), LO2 (BoW/TF-IDF), LO3 (embeddings/PCA), LO4 (LSTM/vanishing gradient), LO5 (attention/sqrt(d_k)/multi-head), LO6 (GPT/BERT/T5/LLaMA/BloombergGPT), LO7 (three working API code listings), LO8 (hallucination taxonomy, context limits, EU AI Act, SEC guidance); each new section has [B]/[I]/[A] exercises; PCA placeholder replaced with executable Python code.
justifications.pedagogy: Learning objectives are enumerated upfront in the remark box; the five new sections each provide one [B] recall exercise, one [I] multi-step exercise, and one [A] proof or design exercise; financial application examples are embedded in every section (earnings calls, 10-K filings, AML compliance); context/deepdive environments correctly gate introductory vs. technical material by audience.
justifications.style: Notation is now consistent (one-hot vectors uniformly bolded as mathbf{e}_i in {0,1}^{|V|}); FinBERT disambiguation parenthetical correctly cites both Araci (2019) and Yang et al. (2020); code listings use consistent minted formatting with linenos; Looking Ahead expanded with explicit section cross-references; cross-reference sec:intro-classical corrected to sec:classical; wrong citation liu2018 replaced with wu2023bloomberggpt.
